cards.q: ما هو الموضوع الأول ضمن أهداف الفصل؟
cards.a: مفاهيم الحوسبة السحابية (The cloud computing concepts) (الصفحة 3)

cards.q: ماذا يغطي الهدف الثاني في محتويات الكتاب؟
cards.a: مزايا الحوسبة السحابية (Benefits of Cloud Computing) (الصفحة 3)

cards.q: ما هو الموضوع الذي يتناول الصعوبات أو التحديات المرتبطة بالحوسبة السحابية؟
cards.a: تحديات الحوسبة السحابية (Challenges of Cloud Computing) (الصفحة 3)

cards.q: ما هي النماذج التي يتم تناولها في الهدف الرابع من محتويات الكتاب؟
cards.a: نماذج خدمات الحوسبة السحابية (Cloud Computing Service Models) (الصفحة 3)

cards.q: What is Cloud Computing?
cards.a: It's essentially a web-based service for computing resources, including server, network, storage, and applications. (الصفحة 4)

cards.q: How does the pay-as-you-go model work in cloud computing?
cards.a: You only pay for computing resources for the duration of your use. (الصفحة 4)

cards.q: What benefit allows companies to handle fluctuating resource needs?
cards.a: Scalability, which means scale up and scale out on-demand. (الصفحة 4)

cards.q: According to the text, what is a challenge related to cloud computing costs?
cards.a: While cloud computing is good for ad hoc usage, it becomes more costly if you try to adopt it as the only solution for all your hosting requirements. (الصفحة 4)

cards.q: According to NIST, what is Cloud Computing?
cards.a: A web-based service for computing resources, including server, network, storage, and applications, provided as on-demand basis. (الصفحة 9)

cards.q: What does Infrastructure as a Service (IaaS) provide?
cards.a: Supply physical/virtual machines, storage, firewall, load balances, VLANs, etc. (الصفحة 8)

cards.q: What is Platform as a Service (PaaS)?
cards.a: Provides the dev environment like Dev toolkit for example, Microsoft Azure and Google App engine. (الصفحة 8)

cards.q: In Software as a Service (SaaS), what does the consumer do?
cards.a: The application is placed in the cloud by the SaaS provider; the consumer just pays for usage. (الصفحة 8)

cards.q: What is a key financial advantage of cloud computing compared to traditional asset ownership?
cards.a: It eliminates large capital expenditure (CAPEX) costs, resulting in lower OPEX due to a pay-as-you-go model. (الصفحة 10)

cards.q: What challenge relates to local laws that restrict where data can be stored?
cards.a: Laws of the land, which prohibit keeping data on servers not in direct control of the data owner or outside a given country. (الصفحة 12)

cards.q: What technical challenge makes moving resources between different cloud service providers difficult?
cards.a: Cloud interoperability. (الصفحة 12)

cards.q: According to the benefits listed, what does "Scalability" allow users to do on demand?
cards.a: Scale up and scale out. (الصفحة 10)

cards.q: What resources do IaaS providers supply?
cards.a: Physical/virtual machines, storage, firewall, load balances, VLANs, etc. (Page 14)

cards.q: In an IaaS model, who is responsible for patching and maintaining the operating system and application software?
cards.a: The consumer (Page 14)

cards.q: What does PaaS provide to the user?
cards.a: A dev environment like Dev toolkit (Page 14)

cards.q: In a PaaS model, what is the consumer not responsible for managing?
cards.a: The underlying infrastructure and components such as the operating system or database (Page 14)

cards.q: What is Software as a Service (SaaS)?
cards.a: In the SaaS model, the application is placed in the cloud by the SaaS provider, and the consumer just pays for usage. (الصفحة 16)

cards.q: Where are computing resources located and how are they shared in the Public cloud deployment model?
cards.a: They are present in the cloud service provider's datacenter and are shared with various consumers in a multi-tenant architecture. (الصفحة 18)

cards.q: What is the major advantage of using the Public cloud deployment model?
cards.a: You don't need to invest in Hardware and effort in setting up the cloud. (الصفحة 18)

cards.q: When is the Public cloud model unsuitable?
cards.a: When there are local laws that prevent you from keeping your data outside your premises, as it lacks full control of computing resources. (الصفحة 18)

cards.q: What makes the public cloud model a good option for startups and any organization?
cards.a: It helps avoid CAPEX costs related to DB servers. (Page 20)

cards.q: In the private cloud deployment model, where are computing resources placed?
cards.a: On-premise or at the cloud provider's premises but dedicated for consumers. (Page 20)

cards.q: Which service is described as an IaaS offering to run Oracle database on Exadata machines in Oracle Cloud?
cards.a: Oracle Database Exadata Cloud Service. (Page 20)

cards.q: What is recommended regarding cloud service providers' offerings?
cards.a: Check the cloud service provider's portal to get the latest offerings. (Page 20)
